diary:
Woke up to that gray, drizzly Tuesday feeling, the kind that makes everything outside look softened at the edges. I showered, made a slow breakfast at home in Kirkland, and just kind of ran my morning batch through my head—how the pâte should feel, how the glaze will behave when the air’s this damp. By the time I hit the road, SR-520’s eastbound closure was already turning my commute into a careful, extra-buffer situation. I watched cross-lake traffic bunch up near Mercer Island and tried not to get impatient—just kept reminding myself I needed to arrive steady, not frantic. 

At Éclair Bakery, the rain matched the vibe perfectly. I mixed, piped, and finished pastries for the display, then did my usual case prep and tasting/consistency check. Lunch at The Islander was quick, warm, and non-coffee—my brain needed a reset. After work I grabbed weekend experiment stuff at Whole Foods in Bellevue, then came home and painted while the vinyl kept time. Later, a quiet catch-up at Finn Hill Neighborhood Park, and then I grabbed dinner-to-go from Pike Place Market and mapped out tomorrow’s dessert ideas. Somehow the weather and the traffic only made the day feel more cozy—like everything moved slower on purpose—and I went to sleep content.
thought_process: I leaned into a “steady chef” mindset all day because the SR-520 closure could’ve pulled me into stress, and stress is the enemy of consistency. I left with extra buffer, which let me treat the morning shift at Éclair Bakery like a calm assembly line instead of a scramble. For lunch, I chose something non-coffee at The Islander so my palate stayed sharp for the tasting/quality check later—damp weather makes everything taste a little different, and I didn’t want to misjudge texture or sweetness. The grocery run to Whole Foods felt practical but also like permission to play: weekend experiments need ingredients when my hands are already warmed up. My mood shifted from pre-work focus to a softer, creative calm once I started painting at home, and the small, quiet social stop helped me feel human without draining my energy.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
